Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to look at the example data The assumed normal flow is Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment But looking at the logs some orders deviate from this sequence

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For case 1002 the order is Register Order Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Issue Invoice Receive Payment Thats out of order Credit check and stock validation happen after shipping which is a red flag

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 has Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Receive Payment Shipping before confirming shipment That seems backwards

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 is even more jumbled Register Order Receive Payment Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Payment before invoice Thats unusual Also no credit check or stock validation at all

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe the system allowed shipping before checks due to an override or error Possibly a manual shipment without proper confirmation or a system glitch where the confirmation event was logged late Maybe a pre payment or error in invoicing sequence Could be a high value order bypassing checks or a data entry mistake Now why might these happen System errors like race conditions or event duplication Policy violations where staff skip steps Training issues where employees do not follow procedures Or maybe some orders are exceptions like priority orders that skip checks But the order type for 1002 is priority yet it still skips checks Hmm

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: I should also consider the resources involved For case 1004 FinanceTeam 02 receives payment before invoice is issued That could indicate a refund or correction but the invoice comes after Maybe a manual process override

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next I need to propose SQL queries to dig deeper For anomalies I can check for out of order events missing steps or unusual timing Queries to find cases where credit check happens after shipping or payment before invoice Also look at resource roles to see if certain roles are associated with anomalies Maybe join with orders to see order value and type to correlate with anomalies High value orders might have different workflows Also check resources table to see if specific employees are repeatedly involved in anomalies

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should write queries that identify deviations from the expected sequence calculate time deltas between steps and flag cases with missing activities Also look for patterns in resource departments or roles

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Finally ensure the queries are efficient and use window functions for sequencing Maybe use LAG to compare timestamps and detect out of order events Also count occurrences per case to find incomplete processes
